supersedes_for_decision: v1 (commit 5474cd6) and v2 (commit pending) — both retain timestamps; v3 is the actionable basket after dataset-wide contamination audit
lead_with_contradiction: User pushed back: 'split errors biasing us?' — correct. Audit found 424 drops worse than -50% in the dataset, of which 399 (94%) have NO matching corporate-action record. The CA table itself is incomplete. v2's new entrants MANAKALUCO and GRAUWEIL had recent unrecorded -46% and -41% drops — they are PROBABLY artifacts, not real recovery candidates. Dropped.

contamination_audit.method: any single-day drop < -30% on a stock with no CA record OR with a CA record whose factor doesn't match the magnitude
contamination_audit.implication: Calibrator trained on dataset where ~5% of severe-drop training rows are phantoms; per-symbol bias is severe for a small set of names but ripples into score compression for clean names too via bin-boundary shifts.

dropped_due_to_contamination.reason: Suspect drops -45.8% on 2026-04-27 and -37.1% on 2024-05-27. NO corporate-action record for either. Model likely flagging it as recovery candidate based on phantom drops.
Suspect drop -41.0% on 2026-04-20. NO corporate-action record. Same artifact pattern.
Catastrophic drop -90.1% on 2026-02-27. NO CA record. Almost certainly ETF unit-restructure event.
Bonus 1:2 ex-date 2026-01-16 with proven unadjusted -89.8% drop (CA factor recorded as 1.5 but actual dilution ~10x). Plus another unrecorded -42% on 2024-04-10.

constitution_note: v1 (5474cd6) and v2 (post-rerun) commits remain in git as timestamped records. v3 is what we trade on. The progression v1->v2->v3 is itself the publishable artifact: it shows the system catches its own errors as data updates and audits run.
next_remediation: Build src/agentic/apply_adjustments.py to regenerate adjusted prices from raw NSE bhavcopy + corporate_actions + a heuristic for events the CA table doesn't cover (gold ETFs, mergers). Until then, treat the dataset-wide calibrator as biased upward.
